title: alchemilla pubescens var. intermedia
intro: Alchemilla pubescens var. intermedia, also known as lady's mantle, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its round, pale yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla pubescens var. intermedia are said to have various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps, regulating menstrual cycles, and supporting the overall health of the female reproductive system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to promote wound healing.
